findings.description: Mapping httpx.TransportError to GraphAPIError (R2.2/R2.6) breaks the resilience classifier in resilience.py. GraphAPIError subclasses httpx.HTTPStatusError, which the classifier (P9) evaluates solely by status_code. Since transport-mapped GraphAPIErrors use a synthetic 599 response or return None (for a subset of codes), they no longer match policy.retryable_status_codes. Because they are no longer instances of the specific httpx timeout/error classes, they also bypass policy.retryable_exceptions. This effectively disables retries and circuit breaker failure-recording for all transport-level errors (ConnectTimeout, ReadError, etc.) raised by GraphClient.

The transport_only set in GraphAPIError.status_code is incomplete. It is missing several transport error codes defined in _TRANSPORT_ERROR_CODE_MAP (e.g., connect_timeout, write_timeout, read_error, protocol_error). These will return a synthetic status_code=599 instead of None, creating inconsistency in how transport failures are exposed to consumers.
